title: osc-brand Quarto Extension
text:
This extension (or possibly later extensions in a single repo) is intended to centralize and simplify the branding and templating we use for our Quarto-based projects.
Currently, just our tutorials and our core branding is targeted. Ideally, we will later expand to slides, PowerPoints, and other templates.
The current extension makes osc-brand-html and osc-brand-html+tutorial formats available. They are quite similar except the second is more specific to our website needs for our tutorials.

title: LMU Open Science Center Tutorial Examples
text:
Important



There are essentially no differences between the osc-brand-html and osc-brand-html+tutorial templates, at least very few if any that you will notice on this page alone. The osc-brand-html+tutorial is just slightly more opinonated with some presets e.g. a pre-activated Table of Contents (TOC) and some code tools/overflow options.

title: LMU Open Science Center Tutorial Examples
text:
brand.yml extension
This example shows a few Bootstrap components that inherit the palette defined in brand.yml as well as a customized SCSS file.

Color badges
Primary Secondary Success Info Warning Danger


Alerts

This is a primary alert using the brand’s primary color.


This is a secondary alert using the brand’s secondary color.


This is a success alert using the brand’s success color.


This is an info alert using the brand’s info color.



Buttons

Primary action Secondary action Success Info Warning Danger



Progress


Primary 68%




Secondary 42%




Info 24%




Cards

  
    
      Primary
      
        Brand anchor
        Use the primary color for the main action or strongest emphasis.
      
    
  
  
    
      Secondary
      
        Supporting state
        The secondary color is useful for quieter supporting UI and neutral emphasis.
      
    
  
  
    
      Info
      
        Informational state
        Use the info color for helpful notes, tips, and contextual guidance.
      
    
  
  
    
      Danger
      
        Attention state
        Reserve the danger color for destructive or error-related feedback.
      
    
  



Inline code and links
Here is some inline code: def foo(x): return x and y and z.
Here is a link.


Code block
# | echo: true
fibonacci <- function(n) {
  if (n <= 0) {
    return(NULL)
  } else if (n == 1) {
    return(0)
  } else if (n == 2) {
    return(1)
  } else {
    return(fibonacci(n - 1) + fibonacci(n - 2))
  }
}


Images
Brand images can be accessed with the {{< brand logo NAME >}} shortcode. Available options include:

Icon, default, dark: these are the underlying image labels. However, shoull use the superset of these:
Small, medium, large: small maps to the icon (or favicon), and medium and large are currently set to use the General Logo (and to swap the colors accordingly with light/dark mode–this is the main motivator to use these labels instead of the other ones!)


Favicon
{{< brand logo small >}}




General Logo

Medium
{{< brand logo medium >}}


Note that if you switch to dark mode, the “LMU Open Science Center” text switches to white. This functions not because the actual text color is changing, but because a different image is swapped in. That is, the the medium logo has a light mode and dark mode image defined.


Large
The large logo is essentially the same, but without a dark mode option available at this time.
{{< brand logo large >}}

title: LMU Open Science Center Brand Examples
text:
Note



Note that the  Code button above is not a default for the osc-brand-html template, but code-tools are specifically activated for this page so you can look at the underlying code easily if desired.
